title: HilbertBench Artifact Metadata
description: Metadata record for a content-addressed, immutable data object. This record lives in the catalog, pointing to a raw physical file on disk.

artifact_hash.description: Content hash identifier. Must match the key in the catalog and the target file.

kind.description: Logical semantic type of the artifact. Helps analyzers filter data without opening files.

encoding.description: Serialization format of the raw file on disk. Tells the reader which library to use (e.g., PyArrow vs Numpy).

file_path.description: Relative path to the physical file. Supports optional 2-char sharding (e.g., 'artifacts/a4/a4b3...npy') to prevent OS directory limits.

size_bytes.description: Exact file size. Allows offline verifiers to perform quick size checks before running expensive SHA-256 hashes.

compression.description: Optional compression applied to the file on disk.

created_at.description: UTC nanoseconds when the file was written to disk.

ref_count.description: Number of span events referencing this artifact. Useful for pruning unreferenced blobs.
